source.submodule: Pointer to pointer, Array of pointer

topicTags: Pointer to pointer, Array of pointer

content.problemStatement:
Pointer to pointer (double pointer) is an advanced concept in C++ that provides a way to work with pointers dynamically. \
Just like a regular pointer stores the memory address of a data variable, a pointer to pointer stores the memory address of another pointer.

The main benefit of using pointer to pointer is its ability to add an additional level of indirection. This can be helpful in scenarios like dynamic memory allocation, creating dynamic arrays, managing arrays of pointers, and modifying pointers inside functions.

Let us check the simplest example of this in the IDE. \
Click on 'Submit' to continue.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq N \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ over all testcases does not exceed $2\cdot 10^3$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of user inputs.
- Each user input consists of 2 lines of input 
  - 1st line is the value 1 / 2 / 3 indicating `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
  - 2nd line has the `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
content.outputFormat: Output as space separated values on a single line - `rollNumber`, `name` or `marks`
